FALL ARMYWORM.current_treatment: Fall Army worms  - MAIZE, Practice regular farm sanitation eg weeding. Spray with Bypel at 15gm in 15 litres of water using Knapsack sprayer at early the morning or late in the evening. Alternate the chemical with Warrior Super at two weeks intervals for effective control of the insects. Monitor the farm regularly to inform you decision of the insects control.
FALL ARMYWORM.prevention: Fall Army worms  - MAIZE,practice regular farm sanitation eg weeding.  Plant early to avoid infestation by the insects. Intensive Spraying two weeks after sowing is the best practice to control the insects. Practice crop rotation eg ,groundnut. Apply nitrogenous fertlizer early in the first week after sowing for vigorous growth. Intercrop with cassava to encourage biological control of the insects. Gather all crop debris and burn after harvesting. Ask for the history of the land before you cultivate maize on it. Always buy seeds from certified sources before you sow. Monitor problem early to inform decision of the early control of the insects.

MAIZE WEEVIL.current_treatment:
-Keep the farm and surroundings clean clean 
-Avoid late planting or off-season planting and avoid planting new crop near infested fields
-Ã¿Use a mixture of maize varieties when planting; varieties showing resistance/tolerance, short duration or increased leaf toughness.
MAIZE WEEVIL.prevention:
-Monitor 10 consecutive plants in 10 locations of the field
-Ã¿Look for creamy/grey egg masses covered in silk located on the underside or top of the leaves and on the stems
-Ã¿Look for light green/dark brown caterpillars with longitudinal .

FUSARIUM WILT.current_treatment:
Fusarium wilt of tomatoes - Tomatoes 
-Ã¿Rogue out and destroy plants that show early symptoms of the disease and destroy by burning.
FUSARIUM WILT.prevention:
Fusarium wilt of tomatoes - Tomatoes 
-Extract seeds from healthy fruits - Certified seeds from recognised institution or MoFA.
Avoid planting on land with history of wilt disease
-Ã¿Rotate nursery site each season and maintain good drainage
-Ã¿Destroy plant debris immediately after harvest by burning
-Ã¿Practise long crop rotation - 6 years with non-solanaceous plants (e.g. cereals and grains- maize, millet) after every 3 seasons

CASSAVA MOSAIC VIRUS.current_treatment: Plants improved varieties like abasafitaa etc; Do not use affected cutting for planting

DAMPING OFF.current_treatment: Use certified Tomato seed; Practise crop rotation; Practice Farm hygiene; Use Shavit F

WHITEFLIES.current_treatment: Spray Cypadem 43.6% EC at 50mls/16lts of water; Rotate cassava with maize for 3 years

BACTERIAL WILT.current_treatment: Use cultivars resistance to bacterial wilt.Avoid the cultivation of host plant eg.potato ,tobacco and egg plants on the field and around.Avoid planting tomato on infested fields.Practise crop rotation with nonsusceptiple crops if you need to cutivate the field.

CATERPILLAR.current_treatment: Apply Lamda at 40ml/ 15 litres of water.

COCOA STEM BORER.current_treatment:
Cocoa stem borers - COCOA 
-Ã¿Plug bore holes with a stick to stop adult emerging
-Ã¿Remove and burn all heavily infested branches. Copice trees with higher infestation and cut trees with infestation low in their stem. If holes extend into the roots Remove roots that have holes in them as well.
COCOA STEM BORER.prevention:
Cocoa stem borers - COCOA
-Ã¿Select and use only borer-free seedlings and young plants for planting
-Ã¿Use none hosts plants  for fruit and shade trees when establishing cocoa fields. 
-Ã¿Uproot and completely destroy damaged trees which serve as diapausing sites
-Ã¿Monitoring should be carried out throughout the year on aweekly basis 
-Ã¿Inspect tree stems for entry holes and check for frass at the base of the tree or gum oozing out of the bore holes. Also look for discolouration on stems and shoots 
-Ã¿If in doubt, plants showing discolouration should be cut open to observe a white ?larva? with a brown head and tunnels in the stem or branches
-Ã¿When any bore holes are observed carry out control measures

APHID.current_treatment: Insecticides like Acetastar, cydim super and cymethoate super can be use at a rate of 50mls/knapsack to control the insects.; Use of soap solution (Alata soap) to control the aphids is also recommended.

CASSAVA MEALYBUG.current_treatment: Good farm sanitation; Spray with Acetastar at a rate of 50mls/16lts of water

BACTERIAL SPOT.current_treatment: Remove affected plants from the farm; Crop rotation with garden eggs for 2years; Resistant/Tolerant varieties should be planted

SOFT ROT.current_treatment: Practice crop rotation and apply NPK 15 15 15 at a rate of 20g/stand; You can also apply poultry manure.

CASSAVA ROOT ROT.current_treatment: Adopt crop rotation; Monitor the problem and acquire resistant varities if you want to use the same piece of land; Do not use any insecticide since the cause of the problem is not an insect.

BLOSSOM END ROT.current_treatment: Blossom End Rot - TOMATOES Remove affected fruits to avoid secondary infection. Spray entire plants with Technokel Calcium @40 mls /15litre knapsack. Observe PHI of 7 days.
BLOSSOM END ROT.prevention: Blossom End Rot  - TOMATOES  Avoid excessive application of nitrogen fertiliser. Mulch  ridges or beds .Apply sufficient amount of water.

LEAF SPOT.current_treatment: Crop rotation; Field sanitation and use of resistant varieties; Spray Carbendazim at a rate of 30cc to 15 lit of water; Top cop is also recommended to spray at fortnight interval

DROUGHT/WATER STRESS.current_treatment: Use stress resistant variety. Practice irrigation. Practice crop rotation.

NUTRIENT DEFICIENCY.current_treatment: Practice farm Sanitation; Use Best Fertilizer at one tin tomato full to 15 L of water

MAIZE STEM BORER.current_treatment:
Control weeds properly. 
spray lambda at 30ml/15L of water into sides and centtral whorl of plant.
 Destroy all stubbles after harvest before next planting

ANTHRACNOSE.current_treatment: For Antracnose on TOMATO, Remove all diseased plants and destroy
ANTHRACNOSE.prevention: For Antracnose on TOMATO, Stake  and  mulching   Crop rotation with non solanaceau crops Spray using mancozeb at100g/15 l of water

BORERS.current_treatment: Manually collect and destroy affected fruits
BORERS.prevention: Spray Bypel at 20g in 15 l of water every 2 weeks after transplanting.Avoid excessive use of nitrogen fertiliser

AFLATOXIN.current_treatment: For Mould on MAIZE, Shell immediately, sort and discard affected grains.Do not feed to animals.
AFLATOXIN.prevention: For Mould on MAIZE, Harvest the maize soon after ripening when all leaves are dry and cobs turned down. Store in well ventilated place

SPIDER MITES.current_treatment: Tomato spider mite     Spray with local soap at 150g in 15L of water.
SPIDER MITES.prevention: Tomato spider mite       Do not grow tomato after host plants like Peppers. Okro  etc    Apply Sulphur 80WP  at 50g in l5l of water at early detection.

LEAF CURL VIRUS.current_treatment:
LEAF CURL - TOMATOES
Imidacloprid eg. Punto should be sprayed on the entire plant and below the leaves; eggs and flies are often found below the leaves. Spray every 14-21 days and rotate on a monthly basis with Abamectin eg. Abamet at 2 0-40 ml/16 litres of water and Imidacloprid  eg.Punto at (20  - 30 )mls in 16 Litres of water so that the whiteflies do not build-up resistance to chemicals.Re - entry interval  for both pesticides is 24 hours and pre- harvest interval is 3  days.
LEAF CURL VIRUS.prevention:
LEAF CURL - TOMATOES
Cultural control: Fields should be inspected daily for the presence of whiteflies in all crops, not just tomato plants as the whitefly may have passed the virus onto another crop.Plant maize around the  field to reduce the ability of the whiteflies to reach the crop.
Water with any recommended  compound  fertilizer  to reduce  stress.

EARLY BLIGHT.current_treatment:
Early  Blight - TOMATOES 
-Ã¿Remove and burn infected plant detected early
-Ã¿Carry diseased materials to be destroyed away in a closed container to prevent spread of the disease
- Apply Mancozeb  at a rate of 24-32g/15l knapsack. Apply at 10 days interval.
EARLY BLIGHT.prevention:
Early  Blight - TOMATOES 
-Ã¿Use disease-free or certified seed for planting
-Ã¿Rotate tomato with  maize or legumes  every two seasons. Observe good agricultural practices to prevent the spread of the disease
-Ã¿Disinfect hands and tools with detergent  after working on the infected plants
-Ã¿Practice staking and mulching to prevent the fruit from touching the soil and to improve aeration 
-Ã¿Remove and destroy crop debris after harvest and solanaceous weeds that may act as hosts
-Ã¿Promote good air circulation in the field by proper spacing (e.g. 70-80cm between rows and 40-60cm between plants)
-Ã¿This disease is also found on potato, pepper and egg plant
-Ã¿Regularly monitor fields for presence of lesions with chlorotic margins on the leaves
-Ã¿Dark brown to black circular or angular spots on leaflets with concentric zones
-Ã¿Black or brown sunken lesions on fruits
-Ã¿When the first symptoms appear implement control measures or next season consider applying a protectant fungicide to protect against the disease. Use recommended fungicides to manage late blight

ABIOTIC.current_treatment: Adviced to use carbadism to spray to control fungi infection on Tomato seedlings.

FLOWER/FRUIT ABORTION.current_treatment: Spray K Optimal at 40ml/15lit of water.

FRUIT FLIES.current_treatment: Fruit bores - TOMATOES  Manually collect and destroy affected fruits. Spray EMAMECTIN BENZOATE eg Attack 1.9 EC at 40 ml in 15L of water.
FRUIT FLIES.prevention: Fruit bores - TOMATOES Remove and destroy infected fruit and infested plants after harvest. Practice crop rotation with non Solanaceau crops. Apply Bacillus thurengiensis eg BYPEL at 20g in 15l of water after transplanting.

FUNGAL DISEASE.current_treatment: Mosaic virus - TOMATOES, practice regular farm sanitation eg weeding. Uproot all diseased plants from the farm and burn. Spray with Golan at 20 ml in 12 litres of water using motorised sprayer to control the white flies. Alternate with Alata soap solution and neem leaves extract plus powdered hot pepper put together at two weeks intervals. Monitor problem early to inform decision of the insects control.
FUNGAL DISEASE.prevention: Mosaic virus - TOMATOES, practice regular farm sanitation eg weeding.Use certified seeds to prepare the nursery.  Remove all volunteers plants before you transplant seedlings. Monitor the farm regularly to inform you decision of early detection and control of the vector. Spray with neem leaves extract plus powdered hot pepper put together with Alata soap solution of 120-200ml in to 15 litres of water .

MILLIPEDES.current_treatment:
Garden Centipedes - CASSAVA
- Apply Fipronil (Fipro 50 EC) at the rate of 100mls/15L. tank sprayer.
MILLIPEDES.prevention:
Garden Centipedes - CASSAVA
-Ã¿Avoid Centipedes-infested fields when selecting sites for field establisment 
-Ã¿Practice farm sanitation. Collect, burn, bury or compost plant debris.
-Ã¿Avoid mulching in sites with potential Centipedes infestation

LATE BLIGHT.current_treatment: Elimiate all susceptible crops eg.egg plant and potato.Crop resistance varieties in your next planting.Always spray with top cop or Mancozeb forth nightty soon after your establish your tomato field.Top cop Rate 50gm/16lo of water.Mancozeb Rate 50gm/15 of water.

LEAF MINER.current_treatment: For leaf miner on TOMATO, use Remon 25mls / 15 liters of water
LEAF MINER.prevention: For leaf miner on TOMATO, practice crop rotation

MAIZE STREAK VIRUS.current_treatment: Remove all streaked/diseased plants, dry and burn or feed to livestock if applicable. Grow resistant variety in the next growing season.

BIRDS.current_treatment:
mammals Attack - MAIZE Trap rudent using mouse trap
- Remove all hiding places where rudent hide from the
BIRDS.prevention:
mammals Attack - MAIZE 
Early planting 
Practice crop rotation 
Use seed dresses on the seeds before planting

MOULD.current_treatment: Mould - MAIZE       Quickly remove weevils from grains by seivinng,and dispose off immediately.
MOULD.prevention: Mould - MAIZE       Harvest maize as soon as it has reached maturity. Remove and burn infested maize residues after harvest.In barn after harvest  ,put fresh neem leaves in layers over Grains.

NEMATODES.current_treatment:
For nematode control: Tomato, 
-sterilisation seed beds before planting 
-Use Manozeb to water the seedlings  immediately germination 
-Before transplanting of seedling soke seedlings in mancozeb
NEMATODES.prevention:
For nematode control on tomato: practice crop rotation 
Practice land rotation

STINK BUG.current_treatment:
Fall Armyworm infestation  - Maize 
1. Handpick the caterpillars, egg masses and destroy. 
2. Spray Agoo @ 50gms / 15L of water unto the funnel of the plant during the early hours of the day.
3.  Apply fertilizer for the plant to out grow the damage. 
4. Regularly control weeds in and around the cropped field.
STINK BUG.prevention:
Fall Armyworm infestatin  - Maize 
1. Practice Crop Rotation with non - host plants. 
2.  Plant early in order to escape high pest incidence.
3.  Plant in strips by alternating maize rows with eg spring onions or cassava.
4. Burn crop residue after harvest.

RODENTS.current_treatment: Treatment of seeds before planting in the future with a seed dresser and also ensure the clearing of farm boundaries

CHEMICAL DAMAGE.current_treatment: Apply Lambda at 40ml per 15 litres of water when maize is about 2 weeks old. Practise good farm hygiene.

SMUT.current_treatment: MAIZEPromptly remove and destroy (but don?t bury) smutted tassels, ears, and infected plants before the fungus spreads, to reduce the build-up of the disease in the soil
SMUT.prevention:
MAIZE Use healthy seeds
-Ã¿Use resistant varieties
-Ã¿The fungus survives in soil for several years and infects plants during seedling stage. Use cultural practices that promote rapid seedling germination and emergence: 
	 ? Frequent irrigation for 3 weeks after planting reduces the incidence of the disease
	 ? Balanced soil fertility. The disease is more severe under nitrogen deficiency 
-Ã¿Practise deep ploughing
-Ã¿Practise crop rotation

GRASSHOPPER.current_treatment: Grasshoppers - Maize Spray with Lamda at 40mls per 15litres of water.Repeat after 2weeks if symptoms persist.
GRASSHOPPER.prevention: Grasshoppers - Maize Spray with Karate at 20mls per 15L knapsack when crop is about 2 weeks old.Repeat after 2weeks if symptoms persist.

CASSAVA BACTERIAL BLIGHT.current_treatment:
CASSAVA Rogue and burn infected plants that have just appeared to prevent spread
-Ã¿At the first sign of the disease immediately harvest mature roots and destroy all remaining plants and plant debris by burning
CASSAVA BACTERIAL BLIGHT.prevention:
CASSAVA The disease is transmitted through stem cuttings, use healthy planting materials obtained from plants without symptoms
-Ã¿Plant resistant varieties e.g. Bankyehemaa and Esam bankye.
-Ã¿Disinfect farming tools in bleach (5 parts of bleach to 95 parts of water)
-Ã¿Remove old cassava plant debris from previous plantings and burn after harvest
-Ã¿Avoid planting on land with history of CBB. Fallow previous fields for a period of 3-5 years if pressure on land is low or rotate cassava with legume or grains
-Ã¿Avoid transferring planting materials from CBB endemic areas to new cassava fields

COCOA PEST.current_treatment:
Apply Akate Master at 100mls to 12 litres of water.
Wear protective clothings. Do not eat, drink or smoke during spraying. Thoroughly wash body after spraying.
COCOA PEST.prevention:
Ensure proper farm sanitation. 
Apply Akate Master at 100mls to 12 litres of water at 4 weekly intervals starting August to December excluding November. 
Wear protective clothings. Do not eat, drink or smoke during spraying. Thoroughly wash body after spraying.

SEED PROBLEM.current_treatment: Acquire seed from registered and certified shops.

BUCKEYE ROT.current_treatment: Plant certified disease free seeds. Practise crop rotation. Stake the tomatoes. If all fail spray any recommended fungicide like Metalaxyl M or Copper oxychloride at their recommended rates.

BOLLWORM.current_treatment:
Remove and destroy affected fruits by burying 3 feet deep. 
Apply Bipel at 15 grams to 15 litres of water. 
Wear protective clothing. Do not eat, drink or smoke during spraying. Thoroughly wash body after spraying.
BOLLWORM.prevention:
Ensure proper farm sanitation. 
Plant 2 rows of maize plants as barrier 2 weeks before transplanting of seedlings.
Apply Bipel at 15 grams to 15 litres of water when 1% of Fruits show signs of infection. 
Wear protective clothing. Do not eat, drink or smoke during spraying.

LARGER GRAIN BORER.current_treatment:
Remove the infested one
 Keep watch during drying
Treat with recommended insecticide
LARGER GRAIN BORER.prevention:
Harvest early
Burn the infested residue

1.RESISTANCE VARIETIES SHOULD BE PLANTED

Apply recommended Seeds
Practice crop rotation
The farm should be weed free
Fungicide Treatment should be early to prevent the attack

CASSAVA GREEN MITE.current_treatment: Apply Neem leaves at 1kg/3inch to knapsack onto the plant.Practice farm hygiene

PHOSPHORUS DEFICIENCY.current_treatment: Farmer advised to apply N.P.K 15;15:15 fertilizer in subsequent planting; Not to waste time and money on already matured plants.

LEAFHOPPER.current_treatment: Practice crop rotation; apply insecticides like Cymethoate at a rate of 30 50mls/15lts of water once a month.

BACTERIAL INFECTION.current_treatment: Selection of plant free from the disease will provide clean planting material to limit CBB spread; Farm sanitation by burning of old infested cassava stems and debris can also reduce CBB spread by rain splashes

PLANTAIN WEEVIL.current_treatment: Good farm sanitation practice, Use banana split stem to trap weevils, collect young plantain and cut into pieces, lay the pieces around the plant with the cut end downward, inspect every morning, kill and destroy the pests, continue the process every day to reduce pests population.

FRUIT ROT.current_treatment: Farmer was advised to continue using fungicide but this time appropriately. Field visit arranged.

PHYTOPHTHORA.current_treatment: Use host resistant varieties, buy seeds from certified shops

MOTH.current_treatment: Spray with Lamda  cyhalothrin at 40ml/ 15 litres of water.

INSECTS.current_treatment: Remove the affected plants and destroy; Application of insecticides e.g. Dursban 4E is also recommended since termite attack is suspected.

CASSAVA BROWN STREAK DISEASE.current_treatment: Removalof affected plant from the field

SEPTORIA LEAF SPOT.current_treatment: Burn all tomato remains after harvesting. Practice of crop rotation with cereal and legumes

RUST.current_treatment:
Termites - YAMS 
Apply Deltamethrin based product (e.g. Deltacan at 175-100mls/L. Restrictions:Ã¿Maximum 2 applications per season in the morning and later in the day. PHI 14 days. REI 24 hours. Eye and skin irritant. Highly toxic to bees and other non target arthropods.Toxic to aquatic organisms. Avoid using near water ways
RUST.prevention:
Termites - YAMS 
Avoid termite-infested fields when selecting sites for field establisment 
-Ã¿Destroy termite hill in and around production site before planting
-Ã¿Keep soil moist during the dry season distrupt termite activities

MAIZE
-Ã¿Use resistant and tolerant varieties

TOMATO LEAF SPOT.current_treatment:
TOMATOES 
Monitor field early for symptoms 
Transplant only healthy seedlings to new fields and site nurseries away from tomato fields to reduce seedling infections
Control weeds on the fields,
Mulch field with dry grass to prevent pupation of vectors
Spray alpha-cypermethrin at 7.5ml/15L of water to control the vector population.
TOMATO LEAF SPOT.prevention:
TOMATOES
 Transplant only healthy seedlings to new fields

CRICKETS.current_treatment:
MAIZE 
Avoid late planting,plough land before planting,monitor rainfall pattern before sowing and dress seeds with insecticide before sowing
Allow natural enemies to feed on the insects.

TOMATO LEAF CURL VIRUS.current_treatment:
TOMATOES
Remove tomato residues and volunteer crops and bury or burn,
Transplant shortly after or during the rainy season,
Conserve natural enemies,
Keep the field weed-free throughout the cropping season,
Spray liquid soap at rate 45 table spoons per 15lit of water to control vectors
TOMATO LEAF CURL VIRUS.prevention:
TOMATOES
Remove tomato residues and volunteer crops and bury or burn,
Transplant shortly after or during the rainy season,
Conserve natural enemies,
Keep the field weed-free throughout the cropping season,

GROWTH CRACKING.current_treatment:
TOMATOES -Heavy rainfall or irrigation following a long, dry period promotes rapid growth during ripening, therefore reduce water supply to the plant. 
Fruit exposed to direct sun are most susceptible therefore a simple shade can be provided.
GROWTH CRACKING.prevention:
TOMATOES - Use plant crack-resistant varieties such as Jetstar.
Mulch and water the plants during dry season. 
Fruit cracking is most common on the large-fruited varieties such as Beefsteak.

MILDEW.current_treatment:
ENSURE FIELD SANITATION TO PREVENT THE SPREAD OF THE DISEASES
SPRAY WITH 20g OF SULPHUR 80W TO 15L OF WATER AT 7 10 DAYS INTERVALS

CUTWORM.current_treatment:
PLOUGH AND HARROWING OF THE SOIL EXPOSE THE INSECTS TO THEIR PREDATORS
 SPRAY 10MLS OF EMAMECTIN WITH 15L OF WATER AT 7 10DAYS INTERVELS, PREFERABLY IN THE EVENING

FRUIT WORM.current_treatment:
Fruit worm - Tomatoes
~Hand pick and destroy caterpilars which are usually found underside leaves at initial (lower) infestations
~Add 1 sachet of Porselen (Emamectin benzoate product)to 15litres of water and spray at 10~14days interval
FRUIT WORM.prevention:
Fruit worm - Tomatoes
~Dispose off crop residues: uproot and burn tomato stalks after harvest
-Ã¿Crop rotate or intercrop with non-host crops (e.g onion, carrot) to reduce field.

FRUIT BORER.current_treatment:
Fruit borer TOMATOES
-Spray one of the following chemicals 2-3 times with intervals of 10-15 days: 1 Sachet of Porselen or 30mls of Eforia insectides to 15L of water.
FRUIT BORER.prevention:
Fruit borer TOMATOES
-Deep plough the field after harvesting to expose the pupae to the sun and to birds that will feed on them
-Predatory ants, spiders, parasitic wasps and parasitic flies are all natural enemies of the fruit borer. Do not kill them if you see them!
-Manually collect and destroy affected fruits
-When using a pesticide, always wear protective clothing and follow the instructions on the product label, such as dosage, timing of application, and pre-harvest interval.

BLACK POD DISEASE.current_treatment:
Black pod - COCOA 
Remove chupons and reduce heavy canopy to admit light.
 Remove diseased pods from the farm to an open space and either burn or bury them.
Spray with 1 sachet of redomil to 15litres of water using a motorised sprayer
BLACK POD DISEASE.prevention:
Black pod -COCOA 
 Reduction of shade trees.
 Mummified pods should be removed and destroyed to reduce spread
 Draining standing waters.
 Regular harvesting.
 Remove chupons and reduce heavy canopy to admit light.
 Remove diseased pods from the farm to an open space and either burn or bury them.

SEED ROT.current_treatment:
Purchase seeds from recommended source for your subsequent farming. 
  Conduct germination test before planting

Cowpea Weevils - MAIZE 
Thresh cowpea  and store seeds in PICS bags
COWPEA WEEVIL.prevention:
Cowpea Weevils - MAIZE 
Harvest matured  pods early to avoid infes

DEFORMATION.current_treatment: On MAIZE, 1. Uproot affected plants and feed to livestock

EGGPLANT FRUIT AND SHOOT BORER.current_treatment: practice good farm hygine apply redomil to the crops

BEETLE.current_treatment: Fall Army worms - maize ,Spray  with Lambda Cyhalothrin plus By pelvic at the recommended rate to control the insects. Repeat after two weeks and monitor their control after spraying.
BEETLE.prevention: Fall Army worms - maize , Do not cultivate on infested area. Monitor the farm early after germination to inform decision of early control of the insects.

TOMATO SPOTTED WILT VIRUS.current_treatment: Spoted wilt virus - TOMATOES Spray field with imidacloprid at 5-7ml/15L of water to control thrip and other insect vectors, if vector populations persist. A systemic neonicotinoid insecticide,
TOMATO SPOTTED WILT VIRUS.prevention:
Spoted wilt virus - TOMATOES Transplant only healthy seedlings to new fields and site nurseries away from tomato fields to reduce seedling infections
-Ã¿Thrips are the main vector in the transmission of TSWV, in nurseries plants can be covered with with a fine mesh (sizes smaller than 0.5mm) to prevent access to the thrips.

ANGULAR LEAF SPOT.current_treatment:
Maintain proper farm sanitation such as regular weeding. 
  Remove affected fruits and destroy them outside farm. 
  Apply effective fungicide such as Top Cop at 60ml per knapsack. 
  Spray every 2 weeks interval for 7 months from emergence of new leaves to beginning of flowering.

ROOT ROT.current_treatment: Fungal root Rot - CASSAVA early harvesting of crops.
ROOT ROT.prevention: Fungal root Rot - CASSAVA Avoid water logged areas and plant disease free and tolerrant varieties. Also ensure GAPS in Cassava production.

NECROSIS.current_treatment:
Use diseases free planting material.
Plant resistant varieties like Ampon,Oburoni,etc.
Maintain farm sanitation.
Avoiding transporting diseases  or affected planting material from one field to another.
Practice crop rotation.

RICE BLAST.current_treatment: Blight disease - TOMATOES removal of affected plants with sharp cutter. Avoid excessive Watering and dumpy condition which are favourable to spread of fungal
RICE BLAST.prevention: Blight disease - TOMATOES mulch fields or plants with dry weeds to reduce contact with dumpy soil .Crop rotation

ROT.current_treatment:
1.CHANGE PLANTING MATERIAL,AFTER THREE(3)CULTIVATION
2.ROTATE PLANT WITH LEGUMINEOUS AND CEREAL CROPS.
3.TRY OTHER IMPROVEED VARIETIES SUCH AS AMPOM,SIKA BANKYE .....

MANAGEMENT ISSUE.current_treatment: 1. Good land preparation 2. Hoeing 3. Use Nicogan 2 weeks after sowing at 80 mls   120 mls to 15 litres of water under moist soil condition.

POOR SPACING.current_treatment:
1. Plant at 1 meter by 1 meter (recommended planting distance) in the next season
2. Plant improved varieties such as Bankyehemaa etc. for better yield

COLLAR ROT.current_treatment:
Collar rot - TOMATO 
1. Create channels to drain excess water from the farm
2. Drench root zone of plants with mankocide ( mancozeb + copper  ) 30g per 15 litres water at 2 weeks interval for 2 times.
COLLAR ROT.prevention:
Collar rot - TOMATO 
1. Avoid planting tomato  in waterlogged areas.
2. Rotate crops to non host crops such  as maize for 2-3 years.

CALCIUM DEFICIENCY.current_treatment: PRACTICE TIMELY WEEDING TO AVOID WEEDS COMPETING WITH THE CROP WITH NUTRIENT AND APPLY ANY FERTILIZER THAT CONTAIN CALCIUM EG. SINGLE SUPER PHOSPHATE

WEEDS.current_treatment: 1.(a)  WEED REGULARLY AT LEAST TWICE A SEASON     (b) WEED TWO WEEKS AFTER CROP SPROUTS, BEFORE THE STRIGA REACHES THE ROOT OF THE CROP     (c) WEED AGAIN SIX TO EIGHT WEEKS BEFORE SPROUTING 2. PLANT RESISTANT VARIETIES OF COWPEA,GROUNDNUT AND SORGHUM TO CONTROL THE WEED. 3. APPLY NITROGEN FERTILIZER TO INCREASE TH NUTRIENT IN THE SOIL

UNKNOWN.current_treatment: Practice crop rotation, acquire Seeds at the approved seed sales points.

BUSHFIRE.current_treatment:
The farm should be cleared of weeds before the drought period commence
Fire  belt should be constructed around the farm to prevent fire from entering the farm

TERMITE.current_treatment: Harvest early and gather the stalks and burn after harvesting.
TERMITE.prevention: Look for  ant -Hill around the farm and destroy it.

MICRONUTRIENT DEFICIENCY.current_treatment:
1. Spray a solution of 70g of poly feed and 15l of water on the whole plant,should be applied in the late afternoon when temperature is low in the green house.
Repeat after 10 days.

THRIPS.current_treatment: Apply Acetastar at a rate of 40mls/ knapsack.

TOMATO MOSAIC VIRUS.current_treatment: ROGUE AND BURN AFFECTED INDIVIDUAL PLANT.

VERTICILLIUM WILT.current_treatment:
Verticilum wilt - TOMATOES 
Remove and burn the diseased plants.
VERTICILLIUM WILT.prevention:
Verticillium wilt - 
Avoid planting in fields with a history of disease attack.
Use certified seeds and discard affected seedlings.

WILT.current_treatment: Rotate your crops any time you observe wilt in your farm, by planting cassava, maize and plantain instead of repeatedly planting Tomato. Pull all tomato plants that wiltand  burn or burry. Practice land fallowing for a longer period, do not plant tomato on the same land until the fallow period elapses.

WITCHES BROOM.current_treatment: CASSAVA uproot and burn the crop materials from the field.
WITCHES BROOM.prevention: - CASSAVA change variety by planting improved variety eg bankyihimma, ampong, esambankyi.
